articles.title: Startup Boosts Scale-Up to 1000+ GPUs in a Single Domain
articles.summary: Delos Data is developing a disaggregated server design and Mosaic software stack intended to support AI inference scale-up domains above 1,000 GPUs, with broader availability planned for Q4 2026. The company argues that larger, flexible scale-up domains can reduce latency, improve GPU utilization, and lower cost and power per token for always-on inference workloads.
articles.advisor_relevance: This highlights continued infrastructure innovation aimed at making large-scale AI inference more efficient, reliable, and cost-effective. For advisors, the read-through is that the AI buildout is shifting from training-centric clusters toward inference-optimized architectures, which may benefit companies exposed to networking, interconnects, optical/copper cabling, accelerators, and data center efficiency solutions.

articles.title: Pulaski County puts 1-year moratorium on data centers, AVAIO project exempt - KATV
articles.summary: Pulaski County has enacted a one-year moratorium on data centers, while exempting the AVAIO project. The action highlights growing local policy scrutiny around data center development, even as certain projects already in process may continue.
articles.advisor_relevance: This matters because data centers are a key physical layer of AI infrastructure, and local moratoriums can affect the pace, location, and cost of capacity expansion. The AVAIO exemption also suggests that project timing and permitting status may become important differentiators for developers and infrastructure investors.

articles.title: NVIDIA Releases Polar, a Token-Faithful Rollout Framework for GRPO Training Across Codex, Claude Code, and Qwen Code
articles.summary: NVIDIA researchers introduced Polar, a rollout framework for reinforcement learning training of language agents without changing existing agent harnesses. Polar uses a model API proxy to capture token-level interactions and reconstruct trainer-ready trajectories, improving SWE-Bench Verified pass@1 results on a Qwen3.5-4B base model across Codex, Claude Code, and Pi harnesses.
articles.advisor_relevance: This is a concrete example of AI agent tooling moving toward more scalable training and evaluation across different coding-agent environments. While still technical and research-oriented, it supports the broader investment theme that agentic AI workflows, especially in software development, may require new infrastructure layers and could reinforce NVIDIA's position beyond chips into AI software and developer ecosystems.

articles.title: 25MW AI data center to be built in McMinnville, Tennessee - Data Center Dynamics
articles.summary: A 25MW AI data center is planned for McMinnville, Tennessee, according to Data Center Dynamics. The limited article text indicates a concrete AI infrastructure buildout, but does not provide details on the developer, financing, customers, timing, or power arrangements.
articles.advisor_relevance: This matters as another signal that AI demand is translating into physical infrastructure projects beyond major coastal data center markets. For advisors, the key read-through is continued demand for power, land, construction, electrical equipment, cooling, and grid-related investment, though the lack of named companies or financing details limits direct investability.

articles.title: Google folds Display Ads into AI-first Demand Gen platform
articles.summary: Google is folding its long-running Display Ads model into its AI-powered Demand Gen platform. The shift suggests Google is moving digital advertising away from manual placement targeting, audience bidding, and static creative testing toward a more AI-first campaign model.
articles.advisor_relevance: This matters because Google is using AI to reshape a core digital advertising workflow, signaling continued platform consolidation and automation in ad buying. For advisors, it is a concrete example of AI moving from experimentation into monetized enterprise tools, with potential implications for digital advertising economics, marketing technology vendors, and platforms with large proprietary data and distribution.

articles.title: Investors stay calm as AI capex boom eclipses dotcom mania - Reuters
articles.summary: Reuters reports that investors remain calm even as the AI capital expenditure boom surpasses the scale associated with the dotcom era. The headline points to continued market tolerance for heavy AI infrastructure spending despite historical comparisons to prior technology excess.
articles.advisor_relevance: This matters for advisor conversations because AI-related capex remains a central market narrative, with investors appearing willing to support large spending commitments despite concerns about overinvestment. It supports discussion around whether AI infrastructure demand is a durable growth driver or a potential source of valuation and capital discipline risk.

articles.title: Reno City Council to debate data center moratorium, possible future regulations - This Is Reno
articles.summary: Reno City Council is expected to debate a possible moratorium on data center development and consider future regulations. While details are limited, the discussion points to growing local scrutiny of data center expansion, a key physical infrastructure layer for AI growth.
articles.advisor_relevance: This matters because data center growth is central to AI capacity expansion, but local moratoriums or new regulations can slow projects, raise costs, or shift development to more favorable jurisdictions. Advisors can use this as an example of how AI infrastructure is increasingly exposed to municipal policy, permitting, power, and land-use constraints.

articles.title: AI Factories: The New Infrastructure of Intelligence
articles.summary: NVIDIA frames AI factories as infrastructure that converts electrical power into real-time intelligence through token generation. The article emphasizes that as enterprise agentic AI scales, performance per watt and cost per token become core economic metrics.
articles.advisor_relevance: The article highlights a shift in AI economics from model capability alone toward infrastructure efficiency, power consumption, and token production costs. This is relevant for advisor conversations about AI capex, data center demand, semiconductor leadership, and the operating economics of enterprise AI adoption.

articles.title: A guide to Day 1 of the 2026 Robotics Summit & Expo
articles.summary: Day 1 of the 2026 Robotics Summit & Expo brings together more than 5,000 developers focused on robotics applications across aerospace and defense, healthcare, logistics, manufacturing, and other markets. The agenda emphasizes robot autonomy, humanoids, AI-native robots, warehouse robotics, commercialization, sensing, actuation, and the bridge between AI systems and physical robotics.
articles.companies: ["Amazon Robotics", "Universal Robots", "Locus Robotics", "QNX", "Schaeffler", "RealSense", "ASTM International", "Boston Dynamics", "Agility", "MassRobotics", "Tennibot", "Harmonic Drive", "TITAN Robotics", "RedZone Robotics", "Pittsburgh Robotics Network", "Blitz Solutions", "Brain Corp", "Harvard School of Engineering and Applied Sciences", "Symbotic", "maxon", "Robust AI", "Contoro Robotics", "Cambridge Consultants", "Agtonomy", "Semaphor Surgical", "Roboto AI", "ForceN", "Kollmorgen", "Thomson", "OLogic"]
articles.advisor_relevance: The article is primarily an event agenda, but it highlights where robotics and physical AI development efforts are concentrating: autonomy, humanoids, warehouse automation, surgical robotics, data infrastructure, and enabling components such as actuators and sensors. For advisor conversations, it supports the view that AI adoption is moving beyond software into embodied systems, with potential read-throughs to automation platforms, industrial suppliers, logistics technology, and robotics commercialization ecosystems.

articles.title: Why robots still struggle to see the real world
articles.summary: The article argues that robotics demos often fail in real-world deployment because machine perception breaks down under changing light, reflective or transparent materials, motion, dust, vibration, and sensor interference. It emphasizes that better AI alone is not enough; reliable deployment requires calibrated sensors, accurate depth measurement, uncertainty estimation, validation, and graceful failure modes.
articles.advisor_relevance: This highlights a key bottleneck for physical AI and robotics adoption: reliable sensing in uncontrolled environments. For advisor conversations, it supports the view that robotics commercialization may depend not only on AI models but also on enabling hardware, sensor calibration, validation tools, and systems integration capabilities.

articles.title: Amid AI disruption, Silicon Valley congressman favors tech partnerships over regulations - Palo Alto Online
articles.summary: The article indicates that a Silicon Valley congressman is favoring partnerships with the technology sector over new AI regulations as AI disruption accelerates. Based on the available excerpt, the piece appears focused on the policy approach to AI governance rather than a specific company, investment, or deployment announcement.
articles.advisor_relevance: This matters because the direction of AI regulation can affect the pace of innovation, compliance costs, and the operating environment for technology companies. However, the excerpt provides limited detail and no specific company actions, making the investment read-through relatively broad.

articles.title: Metro Council to consider AI data center regulations - WKRN News 2
articles.summary: WKRN reports that Metro Council is expected to consider regulations related to AI data centers. The available article text does not provide details on the proposed rules, affected facilities, timelines, or companies involved.
articles.advisor_relevance: This matters because local regulation can influence the pace, cost, and feasibility of AI data center development, which is a key bottleneck for AI infrastructure growth. However, the limited information provided makes the investment implications preliminary and not company-specific.

articles.title: How states seek to protect users turning to AI for mental health advice - Hopkins Bloomberg Center
articles.summary: The article discusses state-level efforts to protect people who use AI tools for mental health advice. The focus appears to be on user safety, oversight, and potential regulation of AI in a sensitive healthcare-adjacent use case.
articles.advisor_relevance: This matters because mental health advice is a high-risk AI use case where policy scrutiny could shape how AI products are designed, marketed, and monitored. For advisors, it highlights that AI adoption in healthcare and consumer applications may depend as much on trust, compliance, and liability management as on technical capability.

articles.title: WATCH: NJ Gov. Mikie Sherrill reveals plan to place guardrails on data centers - NBC10 Philadelphia
articles.summary: NBC10 Philadelphia reports that New Jersey Gov. Mikie Sherrill revealed a plan to place guardrails on data centers. The available article text does not provide details on the specific rules, affected projects, or companies.
articles.advisor_relevance: This matters because data centers are a key physical bottleneck for AI growth, and state-level guardrails could influence project timelines, costs, and where future capacity is built. The investment read-through is directionally relevant for AI infrastructure, utilities, and real estate, but limited by the lack of specific policy detail or named companies.

articles.title: Meet Our Newest AWS Heroes – May 2026
articles.summary: AWS announced four new AWS Heroes, recognizing community leaders for collaboration, knowledge sharing, and cloud education. The announcement includes a reference to AI-powered tools that help builders navigate AWS re:Invent, but the article is primarily a community recognition update rather than a commercial AI deployment or infrastructure development.
articles.advisor_relevance: This has limited direct investment relevance. It modestly reinforces AWS's developer ecosystem and community-led cloud adoption efforts, but provides little detail on AI monetization, infrastructure spending, enterprise adoption, or competitive positioning.

articles.title: Exploring the Benefits of AI Bots for Forex Trading in Forex Markets
articles.summary: The article describes how AI and automated bots may shift forex trading away from intuition toward rule-based, data-driven execution. It highlights potential benefits such as speed, discipline, precision, and more consistent entry and exit decisions in volatile markets.
articles.advisor_relevance: This is a broad example of AI adoption in financial trading workflows, but the article provides no named companies, deployment data, monetization evidence, or investment-specific implications. It may be useful as a general conversation point about automation in markets, but it has limited advisor relevance for portfolio positioning.
